summary: First-run backend integration testing for Lead Hunter & Email Marketing Platform. 29/29 pytest tests passed against external REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Covered auth (register/login/me/duplicate/invalid), dashboard overview (8 KPI cards + 14-day trends + recent lists), full 7-step hunter pipeline incl. Playwright deep crawl on stripe.com (verified [STEP 1-4]+[DONE] log markers, >=6 contacts, confidence scoring rules website=100 / hunter 95/85/70), cache hit on 2nd search (Global DB Check status='hit'), duplicate prevention via unique index, bulk job polling to status='done', companies w/ contacts_count, contacts with enriched company_name/domain/industry/country, multi-tenant isolation (tenant1 cannot see tenant2's shopify.com and vice versa), settings PATCH persist + GET verify, team CRUD, RBAC (Staff blocked from PATCH /settings → 403 and DELETE /team → 403; Owner allowed), campaign create + metrics object, send-without-SMTP → 400, tracking pixel returns image/gif, click returns 302 to ?u target. Found and fixed 1 backend bug (ObjectId serialization on POST /api/team).

minor.issue: Returned 500 due to MongoDB ObjectId leaking into response body. Fixed by adding new_user.pop('_id', None) after insert_one in server.py::invite_user. Verified passing afterwards.

action_items: (Optional) Background task in POST /api/hunter/bulk calls hunter_search(payload, user) directly — works because FastAPI Depends defaults are bypassed when called as plain function, but a search history record will get user_id from the passed dict (OK). However the BackgroundTask captures the `user` dict at request time; if token is revoked mid-flight that is not checked — acceptable for MVP.
(Optional) Consider adding pagination meta (total) to /api/companies and /api/contacts list endpoints — currently only returns array.

(Optional) /api/track/open and /api/track/click silently increment counters even when recipient_id does not exist (no 404). Acceptable for tracking pixels but logging unknown IDs would help debugging.

(Optional) /api/dashboard/overview open_rate computed with `total = max(a['total'], 1)` even when a['total']==0 (then short-circuited to 0). Logic is correct but the max(...,1) line is misleading dead code — minor cleanup.
critical_code_review_comments: ObjectId leak in POST /api/team fixed (was returning Mongo's _id which is not JSON serializable).
server.py is 944 lines — borderline; consider splitting into modules (auth.py, hunter.py, campaigns.py, dashboard.py) as the codebase grows beyond MVP.

Good: All endpoints use uuid string `id` (not _id), tenant_id filter on every query, unique indexes on (tenant_id, email) and (tenant_id, domain) — strong multi-tenant guarantees verified by tenant isolation tests.
Good: bcrypt with gensalt, JWT secret from env, httpOnly cookie + Bearer header both supported.
Good: Hunter mock returns 9 emails, dedupe and confidence scoring (100/95/85/70) verified end-to-end.

Minor: CORSMiddleware uses allow_credentials=True with allow_origins=['*'] (CORS_ORIGINS default). Browsers reject credentialed CORS with wildcard origin — fine for current backend-only testing but the FE may need explicit origin in production.
updated_files: /app/backend/server.py (1 line fix in invite_user — added pop('_id'))
test_credentials: Tests dynamically register fresh tenants per run using POST /api/auth/register with TEST_*@example.com emails (uuid suffix). Pre-existing demo@test.com / demo1234 from /app/memory/test_credentials.md was not used.
seed_data_creation: Each pytest run creates 2 fresh tenants (Owner) + 1 Staff (deleted at end). It also populates global_hunter_cache with stripe.com, microsoft.com, apple.com, shopify.com after first run — these will be hit-from-cache on subsequent runs (test_09 uses force_refresh=True to guarantee full pipeline).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 29 backend tests in /app/backend/tests/test_lead_hunter_backend.py pass. The file is self-contained, runs against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL, uses unique TEST_ prefixed emails per run so it's idempotent. Hunter.io is MOCKED — do not expect real email data. Playwright deep crawl runs against real internet for stripe.com (test_09 with force_refresh=True takes ~5-10s). Bulk test polls up to 180s. SMTP is intentionally unconfigured for tenant2 in test_27. The ObjectId leak bug in POST /api/team has been fixed by this run.
rca of the issue: Bug: POST /api/team returned 500. RCA: invite_user() inserts new_user dict via Motor's insert_one, which mutates the dict in-place adding the generated MongoDB _id (an ObjectId instance). The code popped password_hash but not _id before returning. FastAPI's jsonable_encoder cannot serialize ObjectId → ValueError → 500. Repro: register a tenant, POST /api/team with valid Owner token and Staff payload → 500. Mitigation: added new_user.pop('_id', None) immediately after insert (1-line fix in server.py). Verified by re-running TestSettingsAndTeam — all 6 tests pass.
